'''胸形神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[小山市]]の[[巴波川]]（うずまがわ）左岸堤防の下に[[田心姫命]]を祭神とする神社。[[下野国]]寒川郡の[[延喜式]]内小社。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 {{神社 |名称 = 胸形神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中}} |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[小山市]]寒川1730 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 16 | 緯度秒 = 42.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Tochigi | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[田心姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度|郷社]] |創建 = 564-631（伝） |例祭 = 1月第4土曜日 |神事 = }} == 祭神 == ;主神 * [[田心姫命]]（たきりびめ） ;配祀 * [[市杵島命]]（いちきしまひめ） * [[多岐津比売命]]（たぎつひめ） == 歴史 == == 祭事 == === 毎年1月第4土曜日 === == 文化財 == === 無形民俗文化財（市指定） === * 花桶かつぎ{{cite web |url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=小山市の文化財（無形民俗文化財） |website=小山市ホームページ |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == 交通アクセス == * JR[[東北本線]][[間々田駅]]下車徒歩約40分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小山氏]] {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:あわしんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]]

旧[[近代社格制度|郷社]] |創建 = 564-631（伝） |例祭 = 1月第4土曜日 |神事 = }} '''胸形神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[小山市]]の[[巴波川]]（うずまがわ）左岸堤防の下に鎮座する、[[田心姫命]]を祭神とする神社。[[下野国]]寒川郡の[[延喜式]]内小社。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == ;主神 * [[田心姫命]]（たきりびめ） ;配祀 * [[市杵島命]]（いちきしまひめ） * [[多岐津比売命]]（たぎつひめ） == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の在位期間(564-631)、[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]の[[宗像大社]]を勧請。 寒川郡の総社となったが、中世以降は衰微した。
[[延長]]5年（927）、延喜式神名帳に下野国寒川郡「胸形神社」と記す。
[[弘化]]元年（1844）、社殿再建。
[[明治]]５年（1872）、郷社に指定。
[[明治]]14年9月（1881）、本殿を改築。
[[明治]]45年1月12日（1912）、[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定。
[[大正]]12年（1922）、拝殿焼失。
[[大正]]13年（1923）、拝殿再建。
[[昭和]]16年7月（1941）、水害で社殿流失。
[[昭和]]17年10月（194）、社殿再建。 == 祭事 == {{節スタブ|date=2019年7月}} === 毎年1月第4土曜日 === == 文化財 == === 無形民俗文化財（市指定） === * 花桶かつぎ{{cite web |url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=小山市の文化財（無形民俗文化財） |website=小山市ホームページ |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == 交通アクセス == * JR[[東北本線]][[間々田駅]]下車徒歩約40分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小山氏]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:むなかたしんしやおやまし}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]]

[[明治]]14年9月（1881）に本殿を改築する。

[[大正]]12年（1922）に拝殿に焼失するが、 翌年に再建される。
[[昭和]]16年7月（1941）に水害で社殿が流失するが、翌年10月に再建される。 == 祭事 == * 初天神（1月第4土曜日） == 文化財 == === 無形民俗文化財（市指定） === * 花桶かつぎ{{cite web |url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=小山市の文化財（無形民俗文化財） |website=小山市ホームページ |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == 交通アクセス == * JR[[東北本線]][[間々田駅]]下車徒歩約40分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小山氏]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:むなかたしんしやおやまし}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]]

旧[[近代社格制度|郷社]] |創建 = 564-631（伝） |例祭 = 1月第4土曜日 |神事 = }} '''胸形神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[小山市]]の[[巴波川]]（うずまがわ）左岸堤防の下に鎮座する、[[田心姫命]]を祭神とする神社。[[下野国]]寒川郡の[[延喜式]]内小社。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == ;主神 * [[田心姫命]]（たきりびめ） ;配祀 * [[市杵島命]]（いちきしまひめ） * [[多岐津比売命]]（たぎつひめ） == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の代（564-631）に九州の牟奈加多神社（[[宗像神社]]）の祭神を勧請した。寒川郡の総社となった。
平安時代中期の『延喜式神名帳』には「下野国寒川郡 胸形神社」と記載され、式内社に列している。
中世以降は衰微したが、[[弘化]]元年（1844）に社殿が再建される。
明治の近代社格制度では、[[郷社]]に列している
[[明治]]14年9月（1881）に本殿を改築する。
[[明治]]45年1月12日（1912）に[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定される。
[[大正]]12年（1922）に拝殿に焼失するが、 翌年に再建される。
[[昭和]]16年7月（1941）に水害で社殿が流失するが、翌年10月に再建される。 == 祭事 == * 初天神（1月第4土曜日） == 文化財 == === 無形民俗文化財（市指定） === * 花桶かつぎ{{cite web |url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=小山市の文化財（無形民俗文化財） |website=小山市ホームページ |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == 交通アクセス == * JR[[東北本線]][[間々田駅]]下車徒歩約40分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小山氏]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:むなかたしんしやおやまし}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]]

タグは使用しない。

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Munakata Shrine |Image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |Deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Taketatsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former gōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[ja:胸形神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Munakata Shrine |Image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |Deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)

The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former gōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:胸形神社]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Taketatsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Former gōsha]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:胸形神社]]

According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡}}. After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * {{Shrine |Name = Munakata Shrine |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Shrine |Name = Munakata Shrine |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion {{Shrine |Name = Munakata Shrine |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Munakata Shrine (Oyama)-qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Munakata Shrine (Oyama)-qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama
