{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Oyama revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year }}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year }}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama
