{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year }}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year }}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year }}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year }}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=draft}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=draft}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=draft}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year|12=draft}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|12=draft}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}}

The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|12=draft}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=draft}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=draft}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=draft}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year|12=draft}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|12=draft}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}}

The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}}

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島命}} (Ichikishimahime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}}

The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|12=draft}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=draft}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=draft}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=draft}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year|12=draft}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|12=draft}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|qq=draft}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year|qq=draft}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1844|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1881|qq=}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamada Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] [[Category:City-designated intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Munekata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1844|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1881|qq=}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamada Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Oyama|ja|小山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1844|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1881|qq=}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|ja|間々田駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamada Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1844|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1881|qq=}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=}} in {{ill|1912|ja|明治45年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja|昭和16年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|ja||1923年|1=1923|2=ja|3=1923年|lt=Taisho 11|lt_ja=大正11年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and flooding in {{ill|1941|ja||1941年|1=1941|2=ja|3=1941年|lt=Showa 16|lt_ja=昭和16年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1941年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1844|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Oyama]] [[Category:History of Oyama]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|ja|弘化元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1844|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]
