Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社|version=21968474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Removed translated page templates]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]]

revision.comment: 58 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:胸形神社 (小山市) up to rev 98251866
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 胸形神社 |画像 = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]胸形神社拝殿 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[小山市]]寒川1730 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 16 | 緯度秒 = 42.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Tochigi | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[田心姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度|郷社]] |創建 = 564-631年（伝） |例祭 = 1月第4土曜日 |神事 = }} '''胸形神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[小山市]]の[[巴波川]]（うずまがわ）左岸堤防の下に鎮座する、[[田心姫命]]を祭神とする神社。[[下野国]][[寒川郡 (栃木県)|寒川郡]]の[[延喜式]]内小社。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == ;主神 * [[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たきりびめ） ;配祀 * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島命]]（いちきしまひめ） * [[タギツヒメ|多岐津比売命]]（たぎつひめ） == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の代（564-631）に九州の牟奈加多神社（[[宗像神社]]）の祭神を勧請し、寒川郡の総社となった。[[平安時代]]中期の『延喜式神名帳』には「下野国寒川郡 胸形神社」と記載され、式内社に列している。中世以降は衰微したが、[[弘化]]元年（1844年）に社殿が再建される。 [[明治]]の近代社格制度では郷社に列し、[[明治]]14年（1881年）9月に本殿を改築する。明治45年（1912年）1月12日に[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定される。[[大正]]12年（1922年）に拝殿が焼失するが、翌年に再建される。[[昭和]]16年（1941年）7月に水害で社殿が流失するが、翌年10月に再建される。 == 祭事 == * 初天神（1月第4土曜日） == 文化財 == === 無形民俗文化財（市指定） === * 花桶かつぎ{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=小山市の文化財（無形民俗文化財） |website=小山市ホームページ |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == 交通アクセス == * JR[[東北本線]][[間々田駅]]下車徒歩約40分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小山氏]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:むなかたしんしや　おやまし}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 胸形神社 |画像 = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]胸形神社拝殿 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[小山市]]寒川1730 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 16 | 緯度秒 = 42.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Tochigi | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[田心姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度|郷社]] |創建 = 564-631年（伝） |例祭 = 1月第4土曜日 |神事 = }} '''胸形神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[小山市]]の[[巴波川]]（うずまがわ）左岸堤防の下に鎮座する、[[田心姫命]]を祭神とする神社。[[下野国]][[寒川郡 (栃木県)|寒川郡]]の[[延喜式]]内小社。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == ;主神 * [[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たきりびめ） ;配祀 * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島命]]（いちきしまひめ） * [[タギツヒメ|多岐津比売命]]（たぎつひめ） == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の代（564-631）に九州の牟奈加多神社（[[宗像神社]]）の祭神を勧請し、寒川郡の総社となった。[[平安時代]]中期の『延喜式神名帳』には「下野国寒川郡 胸形神社」と記載され、式内社に列している。中世以降は衰微したが、[[弘化]]元年（1844年）に社殿が再建される。 [[明治]]の近代社格制度では郷社に列し、[[明治]]14年（1881年）9月に本殿を改築する。明治45年（1912年）1月12日に[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定される。[[大正]]12年（1922年）に拝殿が焼失するが、翌年に再建される。[[昭和]]16年（1941年）7月に水害で社殿が流失するが、翌年10月に再建される。 == 祭事 == * 初天神（1月第4土曜日） == 文化財 == === 無形民俗文化財（市指定） === * 花桶かつぎ{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=小山市の文化財（無形民俗文化財） |website=小山市ホームページ |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == 交通アクセス == * JR[[東北本線]][[間々田駅]]下車徒歩約40分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小山氏]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:むなかたしんしや　おやまし}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[Category:Removed translated page templates]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[sn:胸形神社]] [[sn:unakata Shrine (Oyama)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|qq=|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|qq=|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}}

The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}}

The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
