Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in [[:en:1844|1844]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in [[:en:1912|Meiji 45]]. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in [[:en:1923|Taisho 11]] and flooding in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]], but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from [[:en:Mamada Station|Mamada Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|WD=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|WD=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|WD=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
[[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|WD=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|WD=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|WD=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457397}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844|1=1844}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14|1=1881}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45|1=1912}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11|1=1923}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16|1=1941}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|WD=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station|1=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line|1=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14|1=1881}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|qid=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45|1=1912}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|qid=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11|1=1923}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|qid=Q5231|lt=Showa 16|1=1941}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|qid=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station|1=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|qid=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line|1=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[ja:胸形神社 (小山市)]] {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:胸形神社.jpg|250px]]Munakata Shrine Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|qid=Q374511|lt=Oyama|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | Latitude = 36 | Latitude min = 16 | Latitude sec = 42.6 | N or S = N | Longitude = 139 | Longitude min = 43 | Longitude sec = 30.8 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Tochigi | Label position = left |deity = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Minor shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 564–631 CE (traditional date) |Main festival = 4th Saturday of January |Rites = }} '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸形神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|qid=Q374511|lt=Oyama|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, near the left bank levee of the {{ill|Uzuma River|ja|巴波川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzuma River|1=Uzuma River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479901}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} and is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11457397}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Principal deity * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Takiribime) ; Additional deities * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == The shrine’s origins date back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} (564–631 CE). According to tradition, the deities of {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451876}} in Kyushu were invited to be enshrined here, and the shrine became the chief shrine of {{ill|Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|lt=Samukawa District|ja|寒川郡_(栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa District (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Samukawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11457397}}. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} records list the shrine as "Munakata Shrine of Samukawa District, Shimotsuke Province." After a period of decline in the Middle Ages, the shrine's buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|1844|en|1844|qid=Q7633|lt=1844|1=1844}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The main hall was renovated in {{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14|1=1881}}, and the shrine was designated a {{ill|Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihaku ryo kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in {{ill|1912|en|1912|qid=Q2077|lt=Meiji 45|1=1912}}. The shrine faced challenges such as the destruction of the worship hall by fire in {{ill|1923|en|1923|qid=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11|1=1923}} and flooding in {{ill|1941|en|1941|qid=Q5231|lt=Showa 16|1=1941}}, but it was rebuilt in both instances. == Festivals == * Hatsu Tenjin (4th Saturday of January) == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property (City Designation) === * Hanaoke Katsugi (Flower Bucket Carrying) {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/kaiunch/661.html |title=Cultural Properties of Oyama City (Intangible Folk Cultural Properties) |website=Oyama City Official Website |accessdate=2019-07-10}} == Access == * A 40-minute walk from {{ill|Mamada Station|en|Mamada Station|qid=Q873320|lt=Mamada Station|1=Mamada Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|qid=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line|1=Tōhoku Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Oyama clan|ja|小山氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|version=98251866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85883271|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
